trivia_bullets.text: Ornithopter was first printed in Antiquities (ATQ, 1994-03-04), illustrated by Amy Weber, making it one of the oldest artifact creatures in Magic's history. The M11 printing (2010-07-16, illustrated by Franz Vohwinkel) is a reprint — Scryfall's reprint field is true. As of 2026, Ornithopter has 29 total printings across 28 sets including Antiquities, Revised, Mirrodin, Magic 2010/2011/2015, Kaladesh Inventions (a premium foil printing), Dominaria Remastered, The Brothers' War Retro Artifacts, and a Secret Lair Drop [Scryfall: prints_search].

Flavor text: "Regardless of the century, plane, or species, developing artificers never fail to invent the ornithopter." The line is a meta-statement about the universality of the flying-machine concept — a direct nod to Leonardo da Vinci's real-world ornithopter designs and the idea that flight is an obvious engineering milestone any civilization will pursue. The word 'ornithopter' itself derives from Greek: ornis (bird) + pteron (wing) [Scryfall].

In MTG lore, thopters (the creature type Ornithopter belongs to as of the creature-type update) were among the earliest devices worked on by the brothers Urza and Mishra, derived from Thran-era technology salvaged by their mentor Tocasia. The Brothers' War narrative — the same story the 2022 Brothers' War set revisited — establishes ornithopters as ancient war machines. The M11 card type reads 'Artifact Creature — Thopter', a creature type retroactively assigned [MTG Wiki: Thopter, via WebSearch].
sources: MTG Wiki: Thopter page, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Ornithopter is a zero-mana 0/2 flyer, making it a premier combo and aggro enabler. In Modern, it is core to Affinity/Hardened Scales and Hammer Time strategies where its zero casting cost reduces affinity-payoff costs and enables Sigarda's Aid equipment chains. In Vintage and Legacy, it enables Turn 1 kills via Moxen + equipment. EDHREC rank 741 reflects significant Commander utility as a free artifact for cost-reduction synergies. Scryfall lists it as Pauper-legal and Modern-legal [Scryfall; MTGGoldfish: Budget Affinity, via WebSearch].
sources: MTGGoldfish Budget Affinity article, via WebSearch
